Reactions.Name: L1_cond_addwp_exit-canyon-run
Reactions.WaypointText: Head out to the Landslide region

Text.Main: Terminator Thorn led one of the earliest assaults into Mutant Tribe territory.  Thorn was known for his successful frontal assaults on Mutant Tribe settlements.  "There is not a fortification that I cannot break."
The �slightly crazy� Dawn was known for her �inventive� use of explosives, most notably the �Boulder Incident� where an entire town was rigged to explode to eliminate one single Mutant Avenger.
Benefice was one of the first Biomek Generals.  Known for leading the "Assault on Linkin", he was never defeated.  Benefice retired and drove off into the wastelands.
Tracker, a loner at heart, holds the title for the most successful infiltration mission of any Biomek Ranger.  Known for his "One Man Standing" mission against the Mutant Citadel.

Identity confirmed: [] []
Key detected in inventory: No

The Biomek Order has calculated a measurable increase in efficiency among those Biomeks who invest in and maintain custom dwellings. For this reason, the Fort Logan studio residence-pods are available to all Biomeks. At present key disbursal is done by General Dunlap.

All interested Biomeks should contact the General near the control tower.

Identity confirmed: [] []
Key detected in inventory: Yes

Access Granted. If you are in a convoy, you can invite them to join you in your residence-pod. Note: only you are authorized to upgrade your dwelling using the provided Housing Upgrade Terminal.

[$emote]NOTE:  If you are in a convoy you will automatically join your convoy in whatever apartment they are currently in.  If you wish to enter your apartment specifically you may need to leave your current convoy.[$/emote]

All upgrades will be implemented when the residence-pod is vacant. It is expected that you will use that time to serve the Order in an efficient manner.

Would you like to invite your convoy, enter alone or leave?
A member of your convoy has invited you to visit their private residence-pod. Do you wish to join them?
Exiting to Malachite Copper Mine
Now exiting to Scrap Valley

--------------------
Reactor Primer
--------------------
[$imp]Reactors[$/imp] are used in the repair and enhancement of several types of items:

* [$npc]Power Plants[$/npc] which power your vehicles
* [$npc]Gizmos[$/npc] such as useable items
* [$npc]Ornaments[$/npc] to decorate and customize your vehicles
* [$npc]Exotic Weapons[$/npc] using strange energies and ammunitions

Press 'Main' for the main Crafting Primer.
Press 'Structures' for an overview of the crafting structures.
--------------------

--------------------
Fabrication Plant Primer
--------------------
[$imp]Fabrication Plants[$/imp] are used in the repair and enhancement of several types of items:

* [$npc]Armor[$/npc] which protects your vehicles
* [$npc]Custom Wheels[$/npc] for all types of vehicles
* [$npc]Chassis[$/npc], actual new vehicles
* [$npc]Melee Weapons[$/npc] for that extra edge in collisions
* [$npc]Hazard Kits[$/npc] enabling a Mek Transformation for your vehicle

Press 'Main' for the main Crafting Primer.
Press 'Structures' for an overview of the crafting structures.
--------------------

--------------------
Control Shop Primer
--------------------
[$imp]Control Shops[$/imp] are used in the repair and enhancement of several types of items:

* [$npc]Projectile Weapons[$/npc] for use on vehicles
* [$npc]Energy Weapons[$/npc] for use on vehicles
* [$npc]Melee Weapons[$/npc] for that extra edge in collisions
* [$npc]Exotic Weapons[$/npc] using strange energies and ammunitions
* [$npc]Gizmos[$/npc] such as useable items
* [$npc]Ornaments[$/npc] to decorate and customize your vehicle
* [$npc]Hazard Kits[$/npc] enabling a Mek Transformation for your vehicle
* [$npc]Exotic Armor[$/npc] to protect your vehicle in rare environments

Press 'Main' for the main Crafting Primer.
Press 'Structures' for an overview of the crafting structures.
--------------------

--------------------
Repairing Broken Items 
(page 1 of 3)
--------------------
During your travels for the Order, you will undoubtedly salvage or find [$imp]Broken Items[$/imp].  To a skilled craftsman, a broken item is not junk.  It can be repaired, if you have the right parts.

The first step to repairing a broken item is determining what parts you will need.  Open your inventory and mouse over the item, the 'fix list' - or list of broken parts - for the item will be listed in the information roll-over box.

Parts are divided into two types: [$imp]Commodities[$/imp], which are elementary pieces such as plastic or salvaged sheet metal, and [$imp]Components[$/imp], which are more complex mechanisms such as a Drive Train.  Commodities, and occasionally some components, can be salvaged from the people and ruins of the wasteland in the course of combat.  However, you will typically only find the lowest quality of each commodity you discover.  

Low quality commodities can be combined into higher quality commodities or components at the [$imp]Refinery[$/imp].

Press 'Next' for more information.
Press 'Main' to return to the front page.
Press 'Structures' for information on Crafting Structures.
--------------------

--------------------
Repairing Broken Items 
(page 2 of 3)
--------------------
Once you have collected and refined the commodities you need, you must find the appropriate structure where the repairs will have to be done.  Look at your broken item one more time.  It should show you the crafting structure you need to repair this item.  Simply find that structure in a town and remain nearby.

The broken item's information window should also explain which [$imp]Discipline[$/imp] is required to repair the item.  You cannot repair the item if you have not been trained in the required Discipline.  Basic Training in each discipline can be bought from the appropriate trainer in towns.  Look for discipline trainers near the crafting structures that service those disciplines.  For example, if your broken item requires power plants and a reactor, you will very likely find the Power Plants trainer near the reactor.

Press 'Next' for more information.
Press 'Prev' to return to the previous page.
Press 'Main' to return to the front page.
Press 'Structures' for information on Crafting Structures.
--------------------

--------------------
Repairing Broken Items 
(page 3 of 3)
--------------------
If you already have the components or commodities needed to repair the item and you possess the necessary discipline at the required skill level to repair the item then you are ready to actually perform repairs.

Take the broken item and its required parts to the crafting structure.  Once you are in range of the appropriate structure the background color of the broken item in your inventory should change to indicate you can repair this item here.  Right Click on the item to open the [$imp]crafting window[$/imp].

The crafting window will show you a list of the broken items you can repair at this crafting structure.  Select the desired item from the list.  Now you should see an image of the item itself in the crafting screen and a display of every component or commodity you will need in a bar beneath that image.  If the images of the components are in [$imp]red[$/imp] then you do not yet possess the pieces you will need to complete repairs.  However, if everything is correct you will then be able to press the accept button to begin the repairs.  Your actual chance of success for the repairs will be shown next to the accept button.

This concludes the basic crafting primer.

Press 'Main' to return to the front page.
Press 'Structures' for information on Crafting Structures.
--------------------

--------------------
Basic Crafting Primer
--------------------
In the Central Wastelands, crafting is done by salvaging and repairing [$imp]Broken Items[$/imp].  Restoring old or damaged technology is considered an art in and of itself, and master mechanics are often called 'crafters' or 'craftsmen.'  The art of repairing old equipment is often called 'crafting' as well.

Repairing, or crafting, is done at [$imp]Crafting Structures[$/imp] inside towns and other installations here in the Central Wastelands.  This town is equipped with all three crafting structures.

Press 'Crafting' for information on Repairing Broken Items.
Press 'Structures' for information on the Crafting Structures.
--------------------

--------------------
Crafting Structures
--------------------
Crafting is done at one of 3 Crafting Structures, the Reactor, the Control Shop, or the Fabrication Plant.

Press 'Fabrication' for more information on the [$imp]Fabrication Plant[$/imp].
Press 'Control' for more information on the [$imp]Control Shop[$/imp].
Press 'Reactor' for more information on the [$imp]Reactor[$/imp].
Press 'Main' for the main Crafting Primer.
--------------------
The Reactor
The Armory
The Control Shop

Welcome, [$class].  Need something refined?  You strengthen the Order every time you process materials here.

Just say the word if you have any questions.  My secondary purpose is to make sure all Biomeks understand how critical salvaging is to our survival.

You can salvage all sorts of material out there in the ruins.  Plastics, glass, metals, even duct tape.  This is good, because we use all this stuff in crafting.  The problem is, the stuff you find in ruins is usually, well, ruined, right?  Usually you need to do a little repair work to the materials themselves to make them workable in your crafting.  We call this spot repair 'refining' because it takes a bunch of these ruined objects to make a functional one.

There are actually four 'levels' or 'tiers' to each commodity out there.  I can explain in further detail if you'd like, or if you'd like I can continue to explain refining itself.

I mentioned that there are four 'tiers' to each commodity, so let me explain what that means.  Let's use Duct Tape as an example.  Out there in the wastelands you'll find [$imp]Salvaged[$/imp] Duct Tape, but that stuff is all frayed; you can't really do anything with it.

However, if you take a bunch of Salvaged Duct Tape to a [$npc]refinery chief[$/npc], someone like me, I can 'refine' a bunch of Salvaged Duct Tape into [$imp]Patched[$/imp] Duct Tape which you can use in crafting.  The amount you need to refine up to the next tier depends on the commodity.

The four tiers are:
1) [$imp]Perfect[$/imp]
2) [$imp]Functional[$/imp]
3) [$imp]Patched[$/imp]
4) [$imp]Salvaged[$/imp]

Anyway, like I said, you can refine the Salvaged commodities you find into more useful versions by speaking to a Refinery Chief, like me.

To do this find the Refinery Chief near the refinery and speak with him.  This will open the Refinery Interface.  

The Refinery Interface will show you a list of the things in your inventory that you can refine up to the next quality tier.  To refine one of these, click on the entry in the list and press the Refine Button.  

This service isn't free and the cost will scale depending on the quantity and quality of what you're refining, so you'll have to pay the Refinery Chief some clink to do the job.  Once the transaction is complete, however, you'll see a bar showing you how long the refining will take.  Once it's done the Refinery Chief will put the refined commodities into your inventory.

That's really all there is to it.  Wanna give it a try?

Hello.  [$emote]The Armor Trainer seems to be sizing you up, perhaps weighing your apparent skill or worth.[$/emote]

Are you thinking of taking up Armor craft?  Repairing broken armor can be a very profitable line of work, especially for an aspiring warrior.  I can [$imp]train[$/imp] you in the art of Armor craft, for a price.

Crafting, what we call repairing broken items or building them from memory, requires a certain amount of knowledge.  To repair armor, for example, you need to understand how armor functions, how it is installed on vehicles, the shapes of each armor panel you make and many other minute details.  Armor Trainers such as myself can teach you this knowledge, if you can afford to pay our price.  When a trainer teaches you his or her craft, they are teaching you that particular '[$imp]Discipline[$/imp],' and only up to a certain point.

You have to build on the knowledge.  For example, your first lesson in Armor Training will give you the capability to reach low skill level in that discipline. To go any further than that first 'cap' would require further lessons.

Would you like to know more?

It's also very important to remember that taking a lesson from a trainer does not give you the experience; it only gives you the knowledge.  For example, your first lesson in Armor crafting will give you a skill level of 1 in Armor crafting.  To advance beyond that you must craft basic armors until you gain the skill level required to purchase your next lesson from an Armor Trainer.

Luckily there are [$imp]Training Kits[$/imp] available for sale that can be assembled by anyone with a basic knowledge of the discipline.  These kits provide some hands-on crafting experience and will advance your knowledge of your chosen discipline -- to a limited degree.

So, do you want to be trained in Armor craft?

Are you thinking of taking up Projectile Weapon crafting?  Repairing broken Projectile Weapons can be a very profitable line of work, especially since most of the basic weaponry a new warrior can use can be crafted using the Projectile Weapons discipline.  

[$emote]The trainer smiles and points at you.[$/emote]

I can [$imp]train[$/imp] you in Projectile Weapon crafting, for a price.

[$emote]The man takes his hand off of a control panel grafted to his forearm in order to salute you.[$/emote]

I can take a break if you have the clink to pay for my time.  Need [$imp]Training[$/imp] in the craft of repairing Power Plants?
